main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Hierarchical Multimodal Knowledge Matching (HMKM)","Object prototype knowledge for object-level matching","Attribute prototype knowledge for fine-grained attribute-level matching","Training-free, plug-and-play module for OVOD models"],"problems":["Open-Vocabulary Object Detection (OVOD) for novel categories with limited data","Ineffective representation and detection of novel categories in OVOD","Difficulty in distinguishing visually similar categories due to lack of fine-grained attribute modeling","Dependence on supervised learning and large-scale image-text pairs in existing OVOD methods"],"datasets":["COCO","LVIS"],"metrics":["F1 Score"],"results":[{"metric":"F1 Score (novel categories, COCO, VLDet baseline)","value":"Significant improvement over baseline (exact value not specified in provided text)"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a hierarchical multimodal knowledge matching method (HMKM) for OVOD that is training-free and can be integrated as a plug-and-play module into existing models.","Introduces object prototype knowledge for object-level feature alignment and attribute prototype knowledge for fine-grained matching, improving detection of novel categories.","Demonstrates that HMKM can consistently improve performance for novel category detection across various backbones and datasets without additional training."]}

methods: Hierarchical Multimodal Knowledge Matching (HMKM)
Object prototype knowledge for object-level matching
Attribute prototype knowledge for fine-grained attribute-level matching
Training-free, plug-and-play module for OVOD models
problems: Open-Vocabulary Object Detection (OVOD) for novel categories with limited data
Ineffective representation and detection of novel categories in OVOD
Difficulty in distinguishing visually similar categories due to lack of fine-grained attribute modeling
Dependence on supervised learning and large-scale image-text pairs in existing OVOD methods

results.metric: F1 Score (novel categories, COCO, VLDet baseline)
results.value: Significant improvement over baseline (exact value not specified in provided text)

novelty_claims: Proposes a hierarchical multimodal knowledge matching method (HMKM) for OVOD that is training-free and can be integrated as a plug-and-play module into existing models.
Introduces object prototype knowledge for object-level feature alignment and attribute prototype knowledge for fine-grained matching, improving detection of novel categories.
Demonstrates that HMKM can consistently improve performance for novel category detection across various backbones and datasets without additional training.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["OVMR (Open-Vocabulary Recognition with Multi-Modal References)","Multi-modal classifier generation by dynamically fusing textual descriptions and exemplar images","Lightweight visual token generator to extract visual tokens from exemplars","Language encoder to adaptively fuse visual and textual tokens","Preference-based refinement module for fusing uni-modal and multi-modal classifiers based on validation performance","Parameter-free dynamic fusion strategy using exemplar images as validation set"],"problems":["Open-vocabulary recognition (recognizing unseen categories beyond the training set)","Limitations of few-shot fine-tuning (time-consuming, degrades generalization)","Ambiguity and lack of visual detail in textual descriptions for category cues","Low-quality or domain-mismatched exemplar images for category cues"],"datasets":["11 image classification datasets (not individually named in the provided content)","LVIS object detection dataset"],"metrics":["Classification accuracy","Object detection performance (likely mAP, though not explicitly stated)"],"results":[{"metric":"Performance on 11 image classification datasets and LVIS","value":"Outperforms recent open-vocabulary methods by clear margins (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a flexible plug-and-play module (OVMR) to embed multi-modal clues (text and images) into vision-language models for open-vocabulary recognition.","Introduces a novel pipeline that adaptively fuses text and vision cues to generate robust multi-modal classifiers for novel categories.","Presents a parameter-free, preference-based fusion module that dynamically fuses uni-modal and multi-modal classifiers based on validation performance, alleviating the negative effects of low-quality modality inputs.","Demonstrates superior performance over existing open-vocabulary methods and naive/text-guided fusion baselines across various scenarios and datasets."]}

methods: OVMR (Open-Vocabulary Recognition with Multi-Modal References)
Multi-modal classifier generation by dynamically fusing textual descriptions and exemplar images
Lightweight visual token generator to extract visual tokens from exemplars
Language encoder to adaptively fuse visual and textual tokens
Preference-based refinement module for fusing uni-modal and multi-modal classifiers based on validation performance
Parameter-free dynamic fusion strategy using exemplar images as validation set
problems: Open-vocabulary recognition (recognizing unseen categories beyond the training set)
Limitations of few-shot fine-tuning (time-consuming, degrades generalization)
Ambiguity and lack of visual detail in textual descriptions for category cues
Low-quality or domain-mismatched exemplar images for category cues
datasets: 11 image classification datasets (not individually named in the provided content)
metrics: Object detection performance (likely mAP, though not explicitly stated)

results.metric: Performance on 11 image classification datasets and LVIS
results.value: Outperforms recent open-vocabulary methods by clear margins (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)

novelty_claims: Proposes a flexible plug-and-play module (OVMR) to embed multi-modal clues (text and images) into vision-language models for open-vocabulary recognition.
Introduces a novel pipeline that adaptively fuses text and vision cues to generate robust multi-modal classifiers for novel categories.

Presents a parameter-free, preference-based fusion module that dynamically fuses uni-modal and multi-modal classifiers based on validation performance, alleviating the negative effects of low-quality modality inputs.
Demonstrates superior performance over existing open-vocabulary methods and naive/text-guided fusion baselines across various scenarios and datasets.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["CORA: a DETR-style framework for open-vocabulary detection that adapts CLIP via Region Prompting and Anchor Pre-Matching","Region Prompting: prompts the region features of the CLIP-based region classifier to mitigate the whole-to-region distribution gap","Anchor Pre-Matching: a class-aware matching mechanism for generalizable object localization in DETR"],"problems":["Adapting CLIP for region-level recognition in open-vocabulary detection (OVD)","Mitigating the distribution mismatch between whole-image and region features when using CLIP for OVD","Improving localization of objects from unseen (novel) classes in OVD"],"datasets":["COCO OVD benchmark","LVIS v1.0 OVD benchmark"],"metrics":["AP50 (Average Precision at IoU=0.5)","box APr (Average Precision for rare categories)"],"results":[{"metric":"AP50 on COCO OVD (novel classes, no extra data)","value":"41.7"},{"metric":"Improvement over previous SOTA on COCO OVD (novel classes, no extra data)","value":"+2.4 AP50"},{"metric":"AP50 on COCO OVD (novel classes, with extra data, CORA+)","value":"43.1"},{"metric":"box APr on LVIS OVD (with extra data, CORA+)","value":"28.1"},{"metric":"box APr on LVIS OVD (without extra data)","value":"22.2"},{"metric":"Classification accuracy on COCO novel classes (with region prompting)","value":"74.1%"},{"metric":"Classification accuracy on COCO novel classes (without region prompting)","value":"63.9%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes Region Prompting to adapt CLIP image encoder for region-level tasks, effectively mitigating the distribution gap and improving generalization in OVD.","Introduces Anchor Pre-Matching in DETR to enable efficient and generalizable class-aware object localization without repetitive per-class inference.","Achieves state-of-the-art performance on COCO and LVIS OVD benchmarks, outperforming previous methods even without extra training data."]}

methods: CORA: a DETR-style framework for open-vocabulary detection that adapts CLIP via Region Prompting and Anchor Pre-Matching
Region Prompting: prompts the region features of the CLIP-based region classifier to mitigate the whole-to-region distribution gap
Anchor Pre-Matching: a class-aware matching mechanism for generalizable object localization in DETR
problems: Adapting CLIP for region-level recognition in open-vocabulary detection (OVD)
Mitigating the distribution mismatch between whole-image and region features when using CLIP for OVD
Improving localization of objects from unseen (novel) classes in OVD
metrics: box APr (Average Precision for rare categories)

novelty_claims: Proposes Region Prompting to adapt CLIP image encoder for region-level tasks, effectively mitigating the distribution gap and improving generalization in OVD.
Introduces Anchor Pre-Matching in DETR to enable efficient and generalizable class-aware object localization without repetitive per-class inference.
Achieves state-of-the-art performance on COCO and LVIS OVD benchmarks, outperforming previous methods even without extra training data.

methods: Descriptor-Enhanced Open Vocabulary Detector (DVDet)

methods: Object-Aware Distillation Pyramid (OADP) framework
Object-Aware Knowledge Extraction (OAKE) module
Distillation Pyramid (DP) mechanism
Global distillation

Object distillation
problems: Open-vocabulary object detection (OVD) with generalizability to detect arbitrary text-described objects
Information destruction during knowledge extraction from Pretrained Vision-and-Language Models (PVLMs) due to non-adaptive proposal cropping

novelty_claims: Proposes an Object-Aware Knowledge Extraction (OAKE) module that adaptively transforms object proposals and uses object-aware mask attention for precise and complete knowledge extraction from PVLMs.

Introduces a Distillation Pyramid (DP) mechanism that includes global, block, and object-level distillation to enable more comprehensive and effective knowledge transfer, capturing both object and contextual relations.
Demonstrates significant improvements over previous state-of-the-art methods on open-vocabulary object detection benchmarks.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["CoDet: Co-Occurrence Guided Region-Word Alignment","Reformulating region-word alignment as a co-occurring object discovery problem","Grouping images by shared caption concepts to discover co-occurring objects","Leveraging cross-image region similarity to identify common objects and construct prototypes","Introducing text guidance into region similarity estimation for concept-aware alignment"],"problems":["Reliable region-word alignment from image-text pairs for open-vocabulary object detection","Limitations of existing methods relying on pre-trained/self-trained vision-language models (localization accuracy, generalization)","Scalability and quality of region-word alignment without large-scale region-text annotations"],"datasets":["OV-LVIS","COCO","Objects365"],"metrics":["AP^m_novel (mask Average Precision on novel categories)","AP^m_all (mask Average Precision on all categories)"],"results":[{"metric":"AP^m_novel (EVA02-L backbone, OV-LVIS)","value":"37.0"},{"metric":"AP^m_all (EVA02-L backbone, OV-LVIS)","value":"44.7"},{"metric":"AP^m_novel improvement over previous SoTA (EVA02-L, OV-LVIS)","value":"4.2"},{"metric":"AP^m_all improvement over previous SoTA (EVA02-L, OV-LVIS)","value":"9.8"},{"metric":"AP^m_novel (ResNet50/Swin-B/EVA02-L, OV-LVIS)","value":"23.4 / 29.4 / 37.0"},{"metric":"AP^m_novel improvement over previous SoTA (ResNet50/Swin-B/EVA02-L, OV-LVIS)","value":"0.8 / 3.1 / 4.2"}],"novelty_claims":["Bypasses the need for pre-aligned vision-language space by reformulating region-word alignment as a co-occurring object discovery problem.","Learns object-level vision-language alignment directly from web-crawled image-text pairs, not requiring region-text annotations.","Introduces concept-aware region similarity estimation using text guidance for more accurate alignment.","Demonstrates superior performance and scalability compared to previous state-of-the-art methods on open-vocabulary detection benchmarks."]}

methods: CoDet: Co-Occurrence Guided Region-Word Alignment
Reformulating region-word alignment as a co-occurring object discovery problem
Grouping images by shared caption concepts to discover co-occurring objects
Leveraging cross-image region similarity to identify common objects and construct prototypes
Introducing text guidance into region similarity estimation for concept-aware alignment

novelty_claims: Bypasses the need for pre-aligned vision-language space by reformulating region-word alignment as a co-occurring object discovery problem.
Learns object-level vision-language alignment directly from web-crawled image-text pairs, not requiring region-text annotations.
Introduces concept-aware region similarity estimation using text guidance for more accurate alignment.
Demonstrates superior performance and scalability compared to previous state-of-the-art methods on open-vocabulary detection benchmarks.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Detection Prompt (DetPro): a method to learn continuous prompt representations for open-vocabulary object detection using pre-trained vision-language models","Background interpretation scheme: includes negative proposals (background) in prompt training by optimizing their embeddings to be away from all class embeddings","Context grading scheme: separates positive proposals by context level for tailored prompt training","Integration of DetPro with ViLD (a state-of-the-art open-vocabulary object detector)"],"problems":["Open-vocabulary object detection: detecting objects of novel/unseen classes using vision-language models","Reducing reliance on manual prompt engineering for generating class text embeddings in vision-language models","Improving prompt representation learning for object detection (as opposed to image classification)","Effectively handling negative proposals (background) and varying context levels in object detection prompt learning"],"datasets":["LVIS","Pascal VOC","COCO","Objects365"],"metrics":["APbox (Average Precision for bounding boxes)","APmask (Average Precision for masks)"],"results":[{"metric":"APbox (novel classes, LVIS)","value":"+3.4 over ViLD"},{"metric":"APmask (novel classes, LVIS)","value":"+3.0 over ViLD"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes Detection Prompt (DetPro), a novel method for learning prompt representations specifically for open-vocabulary object detection with vision-language models","Introduces a background interpretation scheme to include negative proposals (background) in prompt learning, which is not addressed by prior classification-oriented prompt learning methods","Introduces a context grading scheme to tailor prompt learning for positive proposals with different context levels, addressing the unique challenges of object detection","Demonstrates that DetPro outperforms the state-of-the-art ViLD method on multiple datasets and settings"]}

methods: Detection Prompt (DetPro): a method to learn continuous prompt representations for open-vocabulary object detection using pre-trained vision-language models
Background interpretation scheme: includes negative proposals (background) in prompt training by optimizing their embeddings to be away from all class embeddings
Context grading scheme: separates positive proposals by context level for tailored prompt training
Integration of DetPro with ViLD (a state-of-the-art open-vocabulary object detector)
problems: Open-vocabulary object detection: detecting objects of novel/unseen classes using vision-language models
Reducing reliance on manual prompt engineering for generating class text embeddings in vision-language models
Improving prompt representation learning for object detection (as opposed to image classification)
Effectively handling negative proposals (background) and varying context levels in object detection prompt learning
novelty_claims: Proposes Detection Prompt (DetPro), a novel method for learning prompt representations specifically for open-vocabulary object detection with vision-language models
Introduces a background interpretation scheme to include negative proposals (background) in prompt learning, which is not addressed by prior classification-oriented prompt learning methods
Introduces a context grading scheme to tailor prompt learning for positive proposals with different context levels, addressing the unique challenges of object detection
Demonstrates that DetPro outperforms the state-of-the-art ViLD method on multiple datasets and settings

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["RegionCLIP: a method that extends CLIP to learn region-level visual representations by aligning image regions with template-based region descriptions using contrastive learning and knowledge distillation","Bootstrapping region-text alignment using a pretrained CLIP model to generate pseudo region-text pairs","Synthesizing region descriptions by filling object concepts into pre-defined templates","Pretraining on both pseudo region-text pairs and ground-truth image-text pairs"],"problems":["Domain shift when applying image-level CLIP models to region-level tasks such as object detection","Lack of fine-grained alignment between image regions and text spans in existing vision-language models","Inability of CLIP to localize and recognize objects at the region level for open-vocabulary object detection","Absence of manual region-level annotations for pretraining"],"datasets":["COCO","LVIS","Conceptual Caption"],"metrics":["AP50 (Average Precision at IoU=0.5)","AP (Average Precision)"],"results":[{"metric":"AP50 (COCO, novel categories)","value":"+3.8 over state of the art"},{"metric":"AP (LVIS, novel categories)","value":"+2.2 over state of the art"},{"metric":"AP50 (COCO, novel categories, relative gain)","value":"37.7% over published SoTA"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a novel method (RegionCLIP) for aligning image regions and their descriptions without manual annotation, enabling region-level vision-language pretraining.","Introduces a scalable approach for generating region descriptions using templates and object concepts, without relying on human annotations or only the text paired with an image.","Demonstrates that the pretrained model achieves new state-of-the-art results on open-vocabulary object detection and supports zero-shot inference for object detection."]}

methods: RegionCLIP: a method that extends CLIP to learn region-level visual representations by aligning image regions with template-based region descriptions using contrastive learning and knowledge distillation
Bootstrapping region-text alignment using a pretrained CLIP model to generate pseudo region-text pairs
Synthesizing region descriptions by filling object concepts into pre-defined templates
Pretraining on both pseudo region-text pairs and ground-truth image-text pairs
problems: Domain shift when applying image-level CLIP models to region-level tasks such as object detection
Lack of fine-grained alignment between image regions and text spans in existing vision-language models
Inability of CLIP to localize and recognize objects at the region level for open-vocabulary object detection
Absence of manual region-level annotations for pretraining
novelty_claims: Proposes a novel method (RegionCLIP) for aligning image regions and their descriptions without manual annotation, enabling region-level vision-language pretraining.
Introduces a scalable approach for generating region descriptions using templates and object concepts, without relying on human annotations or only the text paired with an image.
Demonstrates that the pretrained model achieves new state-of-the-art results on open-vocabulary object detection and supports zero-shot inference for object detection.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ViLD (Vision and Language knowledge Distillation): distilling knowledge from a pretrained open-vocabulary image classification model (teacher) into a two-stage object detector (student)","ViLD-text: using text embeddings from a pretrained text encoder (e.g., CLIP) to classify detected regions","ViLD-image: using image embeddings from a pretrained image encoder to align region embeddings of detected boxes","Generalized object proposal and open-vocabulary image classification pipeline","Distillation from teacher models such as CLIP and ALIGN into Mask R-CNN student detectors"],"problems":["Open-vocabulary object detection: detecting objects described by arbitrary text inputs, including categories not present in training data","Limited availability and scalability of labeled object detection datasets, especially for rare categories","Transferring knowledge from image-level open-vocabulary models to object-level detection tasks"],"datasets":["LVIS","PASCAL VOC","COCO","Objects365"],"metrics":["AP (Average Precision)","AP_r (Average Precision for rare/novel categories)","AP_50 (Average Precision at IoU=0.5)"],"results":[{"metric":"LVIS AP_r (ResNet-50 backbone)","value":"16.1"},{"metric":"LVIS AP_r (with ALIGN teacher)","value":"26.3"},{"metric":"LVIS AP_r (GloVe baseline)","value":"3.0"},{"metric":"LVIS AP_r (CLIP text embeddings)","value":"10.1"},{"metric":"PASCAL VOC AP_50 (zero-shot transfer)","value":"72.2"},{"metric":"COCO AP (zero-shot transfer)","value":"36.6"},{"metric":"Objects365 AP (zero-shot transfer)","value":"11.8"},{"metric":"COCO novel AP (vs. previous SOTA)","value":"+4.8"},{"metric":"COCO overall AP (vs. previous SOTA)","value":"+11.4"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes ViLD, a method for open-vocabulary object detection via vision and language knowledge distillation from pretrained open-vocabulary image classification models","Demonstrates that distilling both text and image embeddings from teacher models enables detection of novel categories not present in training data","Shows that ViLD can directly transfer to new datasets without finetuning, achieving strong zero-shot detection performance","Outperforms previous state-of-the-art open-vocabulary detectors on multiple benchmarks","Demonstrates that using CLIP/ALIGN as teacher models significantly improves open-vocabulary detection performance over previous approaches"]}

methods: ViLD (Vision and Language knowledge Distillation): distilling knowledge from a pretrained open-vocabulary image classification model (teacher) into a two-stage object detector (student)
ViLD-text: using text embeddings from a pretrained text encoder (e.g., CLIP) to classify detected regions
ViLD-image: using image embeddings from a pretrained image encoder to align region embeddings of detected boxes
Generalized object proposal and open-vocabulary image classification pipeline
Distillation from teacher models such as CLIP and ALIGN into Mask R-CNN student detectors
problems: Open-vocabulary object detection: detecting objects described by arbitrary text inputs, including categories not present in training data
Limited availability and scalability of labeled object detection datasets, especially for rare categories
Transferring knowledge from image-level open-vocabulary models to object-level detection tasks
novelty_claims: Proposes ViLD, a method for open-vocabulary object detection via vision and language knowledge distillation from pretrained open-vocabulary image classification models
Demonstrates that distilling both text and image embeddings from teacher models enables detection of novel categories not present in training data
Shows that ViLD can directly transfer to new datasets without finetuning, achieving strong zero-shot detection performance
Outperforms previous state-of-the-art open-vocabulary detectors on multiple benchmarks
Demonstrates that using CLIP/ALIGN as teacher models significantly improves open-vocabulary detection performance over previous approaches

Neighborhood sampling strategy to form bags of regions from region proposals
Projection of regional features into word embedding space to create pseudo words, which are then encoded by the text encoder of a frozen VLM

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Progressive Semantic-Visual Mutual Adaption (PSVMA) network","Dual Semantic-Visual Transformer Module (DSVTM)","Instance-Motivated Semantic Encoder (IMSE)","Semantic-Motivated Instance Decoder (SMID)","Debiasing loss for response consistency","Vision Transformer (ViT) for visual embedding"],"problems":["Semantic ambiguity in generalized zero-shot learning (GZSL) due to sharing attributes with diverse visual appearances","Biased recognition towards seen classes in GZSL","Ineffective knowledge transfer from seen to unseen classes due to unmatched semantic-visual pairs"],"datasets":["AwA2"],"metrics":["Harmonic mean"],"results":[{"metric":"Harmonic mean (AwA2)","value":"75.4%"},{"metric":"Improvement over previous methods (AwA2, harmonic mean)","value":"more than 2.3%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a progressive semantic-visual mutual adaption (PSVMA) network for GZSL, which progressively adapts sharing attributes and image features to reduce semantic ambiguity and improve knowledge transferability.","Introduces a dual semantic-visual transformer module (DSVTM) with an instance-motivated semantic encoder and a semantic-motivated instance decoder to build accurate semantic-visual correspondences.","Converts sharing attributes into instance-centric attributes to better match visual features, enabling the recast of unmatched semantic-visual pairs into matched ones.","Designs a novel debiasing loss to mitigate bias towards seen classes by enforcing response consistency between seen and unseen predictions.","Demonstrates superior performance over state-of-the-art methods on common GZSL benchmarks."]}

methods: Progressive Semantic-Visual Mutual Adaption (PSVMA) network
Dual Semantic-Visual Transformer Module (DSVTM)
Instance-Motivated Semantic Encoder (IMSE)
Semantic-Motivated Instance Decoder (SMID)
Debiasing loss for response consistency
Vision Transformer (ViT) for visual embedding
problems: Semantic ambiguity in generalized zero-shot learning (GZSL) due to sharing attributes with diverse visual appearances
Biased recognition towards seen classes in GZSL
Ineffective knowledge transfer from seen to unseen classes due to unmatched semantic-visual pairs

results.metric: Improvement over previous methods (AwA2, harmonic mean)

novelty_claims: Proposes a progressive semantic-visual mutual adaption (PSVMA) network for GZSL, which progressively adapts sharing attributes and image features to reduce semantic ambiguity and improve knowledge transferability.
Introduces a dual semantic-visual transformer module (DSVTM) with an instance-motivated semantic encoder and a semantic-motivated instance decoder to build accurate semantic-visual correspondences.
Converts sharing attributes into instance-centric attributes to better match visual features, enabling the recast of unmatched semantic-visual pairs into matched ones.
Designs a novel debiasing loss to mitigate bias towards seen classes by enforcing response consistency between seen and unseen predictions.
Demonstrates superior performance over state-of-the-art methods on common GZSL benchmarks.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Two-stage open-vocabulary object detector with class-agnostic proposals and text encoder classification","Regional Prompt Learning (RPL) to align textual and visual embedding spaces","Self-training framework using pseudo-labels from uncurated web images to iteratively improve the detector","Use of pre-trained visual-language models (e.g., CLIP) as frozen text encoders"],"problems":["Scalable expansion of object detectors to novel/unseen categories without manual annotations","Aligning visual and textual latent spaces for open-vocabulary detection","Training object detectors using noisy, uncurated web images instead of curated, annotated datasets"],"datasets":["LVIS","MS-COCO"],"metrics":["Open-vocabulary detection accuracy","Generalisation to novel categories","Performance compared to state-of-the-art methods"],"results":[{"metric":"Performance on LVIS and MS-COCO compared to previous state-of-the-art","value":"PromptDet substantially outperforms previous state-of-the-art with fewer training images and zero manual annotations"}],"novelty_claims":["First to propose regional prompt learning (RPL) for aligning text encoder embeddings with regional visual features in object detection","Introduces a scalable self-training framework using uncurated web images and pseudo-labels for open-vocabulary detection","Demonstrates that a two-stage detector with a frozen pre-trained text encoder can be effectively trained for open-vocabulary detection without manual annotation","Achieves superior performance on LVIS and MS-COCO with less training cost and no manual annotation"]}

methods: Two-stage open-vocabulary object detector with class-agnostic proposals and text encoder classification

results.value: PromptDet substantially outperforms previous state-of-the-art with fewer training images and zero manual annotations

novelty_claims: First to propose regional prompt learning (RPL) for aligning text encoder embeddings with regional visual features in object detection
Introduces a scalable self-training framework using uncurated web images and pseudo-labels for open-vocabulary detection
Demonstrates that a two-stage detector with a frozen pre-trained text encoder can be effectively trained for open-vocabulary detection without manual annotation
Achieves superior performance on LVIS and MS-COCO with less training cost and no manual annotation

novelty_claims: Identifies that the essential difference between anchor-based and anchor-free detectors is the definition of positive and negative training samples.
Proposes Adaptive Training Sample Selection (ATSS) to automatically select positive and negative samples based on object statistics.
Demonstrates that using the same sample selection strategy eliminates the performance gap between anchor-based and anchor-free detectors.
Shows that tiling multiple anchors per location is unnecessary for object detection.
Achieves state-of-the-art performance (50.7% AP on MS COCO) without introducing any computational overhead.
